codeReview.summary: The core onboard dispatch/drain correlation wiring is present, but I found two blocking gaps: stale request-id retention on dispatch failure and e2e coverage that bypasses the required requester UI flow.

issues.description: When per-share onboard dispatch fails, `pendingDispatchRequestId` is not actually cleared because the update uses `onboardRequestId ?? entry.pendingDispatchRequestId`. This retains stale request IDs from prior attempts, allowing later completions/failures to correlate against the wrong attempt and mutate `peerOnline`/`adoptionError` incorrectly.

The happy-path and failure-path tests do not execute the required requester-side `/onboard` paste/password UI flow. Happy path adopts via `window.__iglooTestAdoptOnboardPackage` (`page.evaluate`), and failure path injects `OperationFailure` via `window.__iglooTestAbsorbDrains` instead of a real mid-handshake abort from Device B, so the specified UI-level behavior is not fully verified.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The protocol-flow-worker skill requires agent-browser for multi-device validation, but this feature's handoff evidence uses Playwright CLI only while still marking procedure as followed. This indicates a skill/procedure mismatch that should be clarified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/protocol-flow-worker/SKILL.md:21 and :58 require agent-browser/manual validation; handoff file lines 31/36/41 show Playwright commands, line 51 has `interactiveChecks: []`, and line 109 marks `followedProcedure: true`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered a repeatable Droid-Shield false positive pattern around `peer_pubkey32_hex` assignment, but this guidance is not captured in shared library docs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoffs/2026-04-23T16-46-48-518Z__fix-followup-distribute-per-share-onboard-dispatch-and-echo-wire__7f11906e-13b5-418d-a85a-eab93492b208.json:104-105 documents the issue/suggested workaround; grep in `.factory/library` and mission `AGENTS.md` found no matching documentation.

summary: Review failed. Runtime wiring largely matches intent, but stale pending request-id handling can mis-correlate drain envelopes, and the new multi-device e2e spec bypasses the required `/onboard` requester UI path (and simulates failure via hooks), leaving key contract behavior insufficiently validated.
